summary: Validated subscription/payments gating and regression flows across backend APIs and frontend UI for Dial For Help.
action_items: Optional hardening: replace sync requests calls in async backend notification/payment helpers with async client to avoid event-loop blocking under load.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py uses synchronous requests.post in async code paths (_create_razorpay_order, _send_sms_message, _send_email_message). This can degrade throughput during concurrent traffic.
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_* bookings/workers/contacts/subscriptions via API and UI during this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All targeted flows passed: user free-usage counting by phone+email, booking 3rd-block with USER_SUBSCRIPTION_REQUIRED, order creation for both plans, payment verify activation, worker pre-subscription enforcement, and admin booking-status regression. Note: verify tests used computed valid Razorpay signature with configured test secret (simulated capture path, real create-order API).
rca of the issue: Earlier backend suite failures were from outdated expectations in test_dial_for_help_api.py (worker signup expected 200 without mandatory subscription). Updated tests to activate worker subscription before signup, aligning with new business rule.
